functional_purpose: Receives DICOM studies and submits them to OpenEyes PayloadProcessor. It additionally writes qualifying Zeiss IOLMaster studies to an IOLMasterImport folder.
business_process: Imaging ingestion and biometry handoff for ophthalmology workflows.
source_system: PACS or imaging device using DICOM

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after source transformation and processing.
main_filters: No configured filter; source transformer removes the IOLMaster file destination unless manufacturer is Zeiss, model contains IOLMaster, and SOP Class UID is supported.
main_transformer_stages: Extract patient, study, device, SOP Class UID, and association AET values from DICOM tags.
Derive identifier type and institution ID from the local AET or configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType.
Set source platform from ImageNet or Forum AET text.
Remove IOLMaster file destination for non-qualifying DICOM studies.
error_handling: No channel-specific error handler; destination errors use normal Mirth processing.
retry_behaviour: Destination retry count is 0; retry interval is 10000 ms.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000; both destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: Production message storage retains DICOM content and attachments unencrypted, creating a PII risk.

evidence.claim: DICOM listener on port 11112 extracts DICOM tags and submits application/dicom as dicom_request to PayloadProcessor.
evidence.location: Kingston | DICOM | da67d2ba-db52-4f50-898b-b534d71450ab | Kingston/DICOM.xml | source connector and destination 'PayloadProcessor API Send'

evidence.claim: The source transformer reads configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType and removes destination ID 1 unless the device and SOP Class identify a supported Zeiss IOLMaster study.
evidence.location: Kingston | DICOM | da67d2ba-db52-4f50-898b-b534d71450ab | Kingston/DICOM.xml | source transformer steps 'Extract identifier_type and institution_id from Local AET' and 'Remove the IOLMaster Import destination if device is not a Zeiss IOL Master'

evidence.claim: Both destinations disable queuing and retries, while channel properties retain production content and DICOM attachments without encryption.
evidence.location: Kingston | DICOM | da67d2ba-db52-4f50-898b-b534d71450ab | Kingston/DICOM.xml | both destination connector properties and channel properties

functional_purpose: Polls RTF correspondence files and their paired XML metadata, then emits an HL7 ORU document message and a JSON document payload to remote services.
business_process: Outbound distribution of ophthalmology correspondence and GP letters.
ack_behaviour: File source has no response; both MLLP senders wait for a response and use MLLP ACK/NACK framing.
main_filters: No source or destination filters are configured.
main_transformer_stages: Move processed RTF files to sent and error responses to error directories.
Build HL7 ORU R01 with escaped RTF in OBX.5.5 and metadata from a same-basename XML file.
Build JSON containing base64 RTF and patient, sender, and document metadata from the paired XML file.
error_handling: File reader moves messages receiving an error response to /mnt/docman/error; RTF escape reversibility failure throws an error.
retry_behaviour: Both TCP destinations have retryCount 0; MLLP maxRetries is 2.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000 and both destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: Production message storage is retained unencrypted; generated HL7 and JSON contain patient and correspondence data, creating a PII risk.

evidence.claim: The File Reader polls /mnt/docman for *.rtf, moves successful files to sent and error responses to error.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes Correspondence | 06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c | Kingston/Default Group.xml | source connector

evidence.claim: The 'Send HL7 to Cerner' transformer creates an HL7v2 ORU R01, escapes RTF into OBX.5.5, and reads patient and activity fields from a same-basename XML file.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes Correspondence | 06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c | Kingston/Default Group.xml | destination 'Send HL7 to Cerner' transformer steps 'Set OBX.5.5 to RTF' and 'Parse XML'

evidence.claim: The second TCP sender creates JSON with base64 RTF and paired XML metadata; both TCP senders target the configured remote address and use MLLP framing with queues disabled.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes Correspondence | 06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c | Kingston/Default Group.xml | destination 'Send JSON to DocMan' transformer and both TCP destination connector properties

functional_purpose: Receives DICOM on its own listener and posts each study directly to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor queue as a dicom_request.
business_process: General ophthalmic imaging submission for asynchronous processing.
source_system: PACS or imaging device using DICOM
ack_behaviour: DICOM source response variable is None; it responds after processing.
main_filters: No filters or transformer steps are configured.
main_transformer_stages: Pass DICOM message unchanged to HTTP destination as application/dicom.
error_handling: No channel-specific error handler is configured.
retry_behaviour: Destination retry count is 0; retry interval is 10000 ms.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000 and destination queueEnabled is false.
logging: Production message storage retains DICOM content and attachments unencrypted, creating a PII risk.

evidence.claim: A DICOM Listener on port 11119 with application entity OEDICOM receives DICOM messages without transformations or filters.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes DICOM Channel | ba5419a3-24a5-48a2-bd9b-5cb2072e87f4 | Kingston/Default Group.xml | source connector

evidence.claim: Its sole HTTP Sender POSTs DICOMMESSAGE as application/dicom to /api/v1/request/queue/add with request_type=dicom_request and HTTP Basic authentication.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes DICOM Channel | ba5419a3-24a5-48a2-bd9b-5cb2072e87f4 | Kingston/Default Group.xml | destination 'Destination 1' connector

evidence.claim: The destination disables queuing and retries, and channel properties retain production DICOM attachments without encryption.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes DICOM Channel | ba5419a3-24a5-48a2-bd9b-5cb2072e87f4 | Kingston/Default Group.xml | destination connector properties and channel properties

functional_purpose: Receives DICOM on a dedicated listener, writes it to the biometry import folder, and submits it to PayloadProcessor with a BIOMETRY_CHANNEL system message.
business_process: IOLMaster biometry import and asynchronous OpenEyes processing.
source_system: IOLMaster or other biometry DICOM sender
ack_behaviour: DICOM source response variable is None; it responds after processing.
main_filters: No filters or transformer steps are configured.
main_transformer_stages: Write the received DICOM message to /mnt/dicom with append enabled.
POST the same DICOM message to PayloadProcessor as a dicom_request marked BIOMETRY_CHANNEL.
error_handling: No channel-specific error handler is configured.
retry_behaviour: Both destinations have retryCount 0; retry interval is 10000 ms.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000 and both destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: Production message storage retains DICOM content and attachments unencrypted, creating a PII risk.

evidence.claim: A separate DICOM Listener on port 11118 uses application entity OEDICOM2 and has no transformer or filter steps.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes DICOM IOLMaster Channel | eee5caa4-786f-451b-9a89-f00aeb46f935 | Kingston/Default Group.xml | source connector

evidence.claim: Destination 1 writes DICOMMESSAGE to /mnt/dicom with outputAppend true.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes DICOM IOLMaster Channel | eee5caa4-786f-451b-9a89-f00aeb46f935 | Kingston/Default Group.xml | destination 'Destination 1' File Writer

evidence.claim: Destination 2 POSTs application/dicom to /api/v1/request/queue/add with request_type=dicom_request and system_message=BIOMETRY_CHANNEL using HTTP Basic authentication; both destinations disable queues and retries.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes DICOM IOLMaster Channel | eee5caa4-786f-451b-9a89-f00aeb46f935 | Kingston/Default Group.xml | destination 'Destination 2' HTTP Sender and both destination connector properties

functional_purpose: Receives inbound HL7 ADT messages and updates OpenEyes patients and appointments through PASAPI V2. It deletes appointments for cancellation and merge events.
business_process: PAS demographic and clinic-list synchronisation into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: MLLP source auto-generates HL7 AA before processing; configured error and rejection ACK codes are AE and AR.
main_filters: Appointment PUT requires valid date, time, clinic code, and A01, A05, or A08; A08 CHKO and DNA are excluded.
Appointment DELETE accepts only A11 or A38.
main_transformer_stages: Transform HL7 PID and PD1 fields to Patient XML and derive hospital number.
Derive visit ID, date, time, clinic, appointment type, and status from PV1/PV2.
PUT qualifying appointments after the patient response supplies PatientId.
DELETE appointment resources for A11 and A38.
error_handling: Patient response warnings other than missing GP or practice set responseStatus to ERROR; appointment delete treats 'Could not find resource for external Id' as SENT.
retry_behaviour: All HTTP destinations have retryCount 0; retry interval is 10000 ms.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000 and all HTTP destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: Transformer scripts log caught mapping exceptions; production message storage and HospitalNumber metadata retain PII unencrypted.

evidence.claim: The TCP Listener accepts MLLP HL7v2 on port 6662 and auto-generates the response before processing; its response settings define AA, AE, and AR ACK codes.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes PAS | 6c3a10d1-968d-468e-9355-008946825b20 | Kingston/PAS.xml | source connector and HL7v2 response generation properties

evidence.claim: The patient destination PUTs XSLT-produced Patient XML to PASAPI/V2/Patient/${HospitalNumber} with HTTP Basic authentication, and its response transformer records PatientId while escalating relevant warnings.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes PAS | 6c3a10d1-968d-468e-9355-008946825b20 | Kingston/PAS.xml | destination 'PASAPI - Patient' connector, transformer, and response transformer

evidence.claim: Appointment PUT filters A01, A05, and qualifying A08 messages, while appointment DELETE filters A11 and A38 and sends to PASAPI/V2/PatientAppointment/${VisitID}.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes PAS | 6c3a10d1-968d-468e-9355-008946825b20 | Kingston/PAS.xml | destinations 'Clinic List - PUT' and 'Clinic List - DELETE' filters and connectors

evidence.claim: All HTTP destinations disable queuing and retries; mapping scripts log errors and channel properties store HospitalNumber as metadata with production content retention unencrypted.
evidence.location: Kingston | OpenEyes PAS | 6c3a10d1-968d-468e-9355-008946825b20 | Kingston/PAS.xml | destination connector properties, transformer scripts, and channel properties
